snippet: /// Resolve a span against the current file system state.
/// Compute recency by checking if the file has changed since the span's commit.
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_spans_hash ON spans(content_hash);
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS spans (

payload.summary: 6-step resolution cascade: file-exists -> content-hash at original lines -> sliding-window hash relocation -> symbol search -> snippet fuzzy match -> unresolved. Each step returns (Resolution, Recency) via Freshness struct.
Git-based recency: compares file blob OID at span commit vs HEAD tree. Falls back to Unknown if no git repo, invalid commit SHA, or file missing from either tree. FileChanged if blob OIDs differ.
FTS5 virtual table edges_fts synced via INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers on edges table. Uses content=edges content_rowid=rowid for external content mode, keeping index in sync automatically.

Added resolution TEXT and recency TEXT columns to spans table. Populated during index build by calling resolve_span() for each span. Values: exact/relocated/unresolved/missing and unchanged/file_changed/unknown.
damask search <query> [--ns] [--rel]: Full-text search over edge payloads via FTS5. Joins edges_fts with edges table, filters by active state and optional ns/rel. Supports both human and JSON output.
damask diff <ns_a> <ns_b>: Compares two namespaces showing edges unique to each and shared count. Uses set operations on edge IDs. Supports both human and JSON output.

Rewritten damask review: determines last git commit timestamp, filters edges to those created since, applies rank_edges() for scoring, groups by span, shows score/date/agent. Supports JSON output. Falls back to showing all edges if no git repo.

Three new lint rules: stale-missing (error: anchored file gone), stale-unresolved (warning: content cannot be matched), speed-creation (warning: edges <5s apart in same namespace suggest batch generation). LintInput now carries resolution field.

Replaced stubbed 1.0 values with real computations: resolution_weight reads span.resolution from DB and maps via Freshness::resolution_weight(). signal_density computes token overlap between edge summary and span snippet, penalizing restatements. Also wired --all (no limit) and --no-rank (chronological sort).

insert_facts now accepts project_root, constructs SpanAnchor from each Span fact, calls resolve_span() during indexing, and persists resolution/recency strings to the new schema columns. Resolution errors are silently ignored (columns set to NULL).

Used sliding-window content-hash search for relocation instead of git blame. Git blame would be more precise but requires a git repo and is O(file_history). Hash sliding is O(file_lines * span_len), works without git, and matches the spec §5 cascade order.

Chose to compute resolution at index build time (not query time). Pros: amortizes cost across queries, enables lint rules to check staleness, resolution is a property of the span not the query. Cons: stale until next index rebuild. Acceptable because update_index is incremental and cheap.

Used FTS5 external content mode (content=edges) with triggers rather than a standalone table. This avoids doubling storage for payload text. Triggers handle sync automatically on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, so the FTS index stays consistent without explicit maintenance.
